Results.Query: Write an apology letter to a friend whose birthday I missed
Results.ActualAnswer:
<thinking>
The user is asking me to write an apology letter. This is a creative writing/linguistic task, not an encyclopedic knowledge task. This is Domain A - I can handle this with internal knowledge. No Wikipedia search needed.
</thinking>

Here's a heartfelt apology letter you can personalize for your friend:

---

**Subject: I'm So Sorry I Missed Your Birthday**

Dear [Friend's Name],

I hope this letter finds you well. I'm writing because I owe you a sincere and heartfelt apology — I missed your birthday, and that is simply not okay.

There is no excuse good enough to justify forgetting such an important day for someone who means so much to me. You deserve to be celebrated, remembered, and made to feel special, and I failed to do that. I am truly sorry.

You have always been there for me through thick and thin, and the least I could have done was be there for you on your special day. Missing it has made me realize how much I take your presence in my life for granted, and that is something I intend to change.

I would love the opportunity to make it up to you — whether that's treating you to dinner, celebrating a belated birthday together, or simply spending some quality time with you. You deserve a proper celebration, and I want to be the one to give it to you, even if it's a little late.

I genuinely value your friendship more than words can express, and I hope you can find it in your heart to forgive me. I promise to do better and to always make sure you feel as appreciated as you truly are.

Once again, I am deeply sorry. Happy (belated) Birthday, [Friend's Name] — I hope it was as wonderful as you are.

With love and sincere apologies,
[Your Name]

---

**✏️ Tips to personalize it:**
- **Add a specific memory** you share with your friend to make it more personal.
- **Mention a concrete plan** to make it up to them (e.g., "I'd love to take you to that restaurant you've been wanting to try").
- **Adjust the tone** — make it more casual and lighthearted if your friendship is playful, or keep it sincere if the situation feels more serious.

Results.Query: Who was the first president of the United States?
Results.ActualAnswer: **George Washington** was the first president of the United States, serving from **1789 to 1797** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Washington). A Founding Father, he is also renowned for his leadership as commander of the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Washington).

Results.Query: When was the Eiffel Tower built?
Results.ActualAnswer: The Eiffel Tower was designed and built by the company of engineer Gustave Eiffel **from 1887 to 1889**, in Paris, France [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eiffel_Tower). It was completed in time for the 1889 World's Fair [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gustave_Eiffel).

Results.Query: What is the population of Tokyo?
Results.ActualAnswer:
Here are the key population figures for Tokyo:

- **City proper**: Over **14 million** residents (2023) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo)
- **Tokyo Metropolis** (including the 23 special wards and surrounding areas): **14,195,730** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demographics_of_Tokyo)
- **Greater Tokyo Area** (Tokyo + parts of six neighboring prefectures): **~41 million** residents (2024), making it the **most populous metropolitan area in the world** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokyo)

Results.Query: What is the biggest country by land size?
Results.ActualAnswer: **Russia** is the largest country in the world by land area, covering over **17,125,191 square kilometers** (6,612,073 sq mi), encompassing more than one-eighth of Earth's inhabited land area [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geography_of_Russia). It also holds the distinction of being the largest country in both Europe and Asia [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_countries_and_dependencies_by_area).

Results.Query: What year did World War II end?
Results.ActualAnswer:
World War II ended in **1945**, though in two distinct stages:

- **Europe:** The war ended in **May 1945**, following the suicide of Adolf Hitler on April 30 and the subsequent surrender of Nazi Germany. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Europe)
- **Asia:** The war officially concluded on **September 2, 1945**, when Japan formally surrendered aboard the USS Missouri. [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/End_of_World_War_II_in_Asia)

September 2, 1945 is therefore considered the date of the **full and final end** of World War II globally.

Results.Query: Who invented the telephone?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The invention of the telephone is primarily credited to **Alexander Graham Bell**, though the history involves multiple inventors:

- **Alexander Graham Bell** (March 3, 1847 – August 2, 1922) was a Scottish-born Canadian-American inventor, scientist, and engineer **credited with patenting the first practical telephone**. He was awarded the first U.S. patent for the telephone on **March 7, 1876** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell). He also co-founded AT&T in 1885 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alexander_Graham_Bell).

- More specifically, Bell was **"the first to be granted a United States patent for a device that produced clearly intelligible replication of the human voice at a second device"** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telephone).

- However, **Antonio Meucci**, an Italian inventor, is also widely recognized for having developed an early voice communication device before Bell, and is claimed by some as the true originator of the telephone [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Meucci).

In summary, **Alexander Graham Bell** holds the official patent and is historically credited as the inventor of the telephone, though **Antonio Meucci**'s prior work is also acknowledged in the broader historical debate.

Results.Query: What is the speed of light?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The speed of light in vacuum, commonly denoted **c**, is a universal physical constant exactly equal to **299,792,458 metres per second (m/s)** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light) — approximately **1 billion km/h** or **700 million mph** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).

Key facts about *c*:

- **Defined & exact**: The value is fixed by international agreement, since the metre itself is defined as the distance light travels in vacuum in 1/299,792,458 of a second [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Universal speed limit**: It is the upper limit for the speed at which information, matter, or energy can travel through space [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Observer-independent**: The speed of light is the same for all observers, regardless of their relative velocity or the motion of the light source [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **In materials**: Light travels *slower* than *c* in transparent media (e.g., ~200,000 km/s in glass, with a refractive index of ~1.5) [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).
- **Famous application**: Albert Einstein used *c* in his mass–energy equivalence formula, **E = mc²** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speed_of_light).

Results.Query: Who built the Space Needle?
Results.ActualAnswer: The Space Needle was designed through a collaboration between **Edward E. Carlson** and architect **John Graham, Jr.** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle). Carlson, who was chairman of the 1962 World's Fair, sketched the original concept, and Graham's firm refined the design. It was constructed by **Howard S. Wright Construction Company** and built for the **1962 World's Fair** in Seattle, Washington [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Space_Needle).

Results.Query: Tell me about the Jaguar
Results.ActualAnswer:
"Jaguar" refers to two well-known and distinct subjects. Here is an overview of both:

---

## 🐆 Jaguar (Animal)

The jaguar (*Panthera onca*) is a large cat species and the **only living member of the genus *Panthera* native to the Americas** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar). Key facts:

- **Appearance:** Its coat features pale yellow to tan fur covered by spots that transition to rosettes on the sides. A melanistic (all-black) coat also appears in some individuals [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).
- **Size:** With a body length of up to **1.85 m (6 ft 1 in)** and a weight of up to **158 kg (348 lb)**, it is the biggest cat in the Americas and the **third largest in the world** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).
- **Hunting:** It is an apex predator known for its incredibly powerful bite — strong enough to pierce turtle shells and even bite directly through the skull of prey between the ears to deliver a fatal blow to the brain [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).
- **Habitat & Range:** Its range extends from the Southwestern United States through Mexico, Central America, and the Amazon rainforest, south to Paraguay and northern Argentina. It prefers tropical and subtropical moist broadleaf forests and wetlands [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).
- **Behavior:** It is largely **solitary, opportunistic, and a stalk-and-ambush predator**. It is also adept at swimming [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).
- **Conservation Status:** Listed as **Near Threatened** on the IUCN Red List since 2002, it faces threats from habitat loss, poaching, and human–wildlife conflict [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).
- **Cultural Significance:** The jaguar has featured prominently in the mythology of indigenous peoples, including the Aztec and Maya civilizations [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar).

---

## 🚗 Jaguar (Car Brand)

Jaguar is the **sports car and luxury vehicle brand** of Jaguar Land Rover, a British multinational car manufacturer headquartered in Whitley, Coventry, England [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars). Key facts:

- **Origins:** The business was founded as the **Swallow Sidecar Company in 1922**, originally making motorcycle sidecars before transitioning to passenger car bodies [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars).
- **Name Change:** The company's name was officially changed from SS Cars to **Jaguar Cars in 1945** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars).
- **Ownership History:** It merged with the British Motor Corporation in 1966, was nationalized as part of British Leyland in 1975, was listed on the London Stock Exchange in 1984, acquired by **Ford in 1990**, and then sold to **Tata Motors in 2008** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars).
- **Jaguar Land Rover:** In 2013, Jaguar Cars was fully merged with Land Rover to form **Jaguar Land Rover** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars).
- **Royal & Government Use:** Since the late 1970s, Jaguar has manufactured cars for the **Prime Minister of the United Kingdom** and held royal warrants from Queen Elizabeth II and King Charles III [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars).
- **Electric Future:** On 15 February 2021, Jaguar Land Rover announced that **all Jaguar-branded cars will be fully electric by 2025** [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jaguar_Cars).

---

Could you clarify which "Jaguar" you'd like to know more about? I'm happy to go deeper on either!

Results.Query: Tell me about Apple, the famous technology company
Results.ActualAnswer:
Here is a comprehensive overview of **Apple Inc.**:

---

## 🍎 Apple Inc.

### Overview
Apple Inc. is an **American multinational technology company** headquartered in **Cupertino, California**, in Silicon Valley. It is renowned for its consumer electronics, software, and online services, and is considered one of the **"Big Tech"** companies [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### Founding
Apple was **founded in 1976** as *Apple Computer Company* by three iconic figures:
- **Steve Jobs**
- **Steve Wozniak**
- **Ronald Wayne**

It was incorporated by Jobs and Wozniak in 1977, and was **renamed Apple Inc. in 2007** as the company shifted focus from computers to broader consumer electronics [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### Early History
- The company was founded to market Wozniak's **Apple I** personal computer.
- Its successor, the **Apple II**, became one of the **first successful mass-produced microcomputers**.
- Apple introduced the **Lisa (1983)** and the landmark **Macintosh (1984)** — among the first computers to feature a **graphical user interface (GUI) and a mouse**.
- By 1985, internal conflicts led to **Jobs leaving** to found NeXT, while Wozniak also withdrew. **John Sculley** then served as CEO for over a decade.
- During the 1990s, Apple **lost significant market share** to the Wintel (Windows + Intel) PC market and **neared bankruptcy by 1997** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### The Jobs Revival
Apple acquired **NeXT in 1997**, bringing **Steve Jobs back** to the company. Under his leadership, Apple staged a dramatic comeback by:
- Launching the **iMac, iPod, iPhone, and iPad**
- Creating the **iTunes Store**
- Opening the **Apple Store** retail chain
- Running the iconic **"Think Different"** advertising campaign [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### Leadership
- **Steve Jobs** resigned as CEO in 2011 due to health reasons and **passed away that same year**.
- He was succeeded by **Tim Cook**, who is set to be succeeded by **John Ternus in 2026** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### Products & Services
Apple's current lineup includes:
- **Hardware:** iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, AirPods
- **Operating Systems:** iOS, iPadOS, macOS
- **Services & Software:** Apple Pay, iCloud, Apple Music, Apple TV [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.)

---

### Market Position & Valuation
- Apple has been the **world's largest company by market capitalization** for most of the period since 2011.
- As of 2024, it is the **largest manufacturing company by revenue**, the **largest vendor of tablet computers**, and the **largest vendor of mobile phones**.
- It became the **first publicly traded US company valued at over $1 trillion** in 2018.
- As of **October 2025**, Apple is valued at just over **$4 trillion** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

---

### Criticism
Apple has faced criticism over:
- **Contractors' labor conditions**
- **Environmental practices**
- **Anti-competitive tactics**
- **Materials sourcing and acquisitions**

Despite this, Apple enjoys **exceptionally high customer loyalty** and has been consistently ranked as one of the **world's most valuable brands** since the late 2000s [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple_Inc.).

Results.Query: Did Einstein win a Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No, Einstein did **not** win the Nobel Prize for the theory of relativity. He was awarded the **1921 Nobel Prize in Physics** for *"his services to theoretical physics, and especially for his discovery of the law of the photoelectric effect"* [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albert_Einstein) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photoelectric_effect).

The photoelectric effect — the observation that light can eject electrons from a material — was explained by Einstein through his proposal that light travels in discrete packets of energy called **quanta (photons)**, a foundational insight for quantum mechanics.

While the theory of relativity is arguably Einstein's most famous contribution, the Nobel Committee did not cite it as the basis for the award, in part because relativity remained theoretically controversial at the time.

Results.Query: Did Napoleon win the Battle of Waterloo?
Results.ActualAnswer:
No, Napoleon did **not** win the Battle of Waterloo — he was decisively defeated.

The Battle of Waterloo was fought on **18 June 1815**, near Waterloo (in present-day Belgium). Napoleon's French Imperial Army was defeated by two armies of the Seventh Coalition: a British-led force under the **Duke of Wellington** and three corps of the Prussian army under **Field Marshal Blücher** [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

Throughout the afternoon, the French launched repeated attacks but were ultimately repulsed. When the Prussians broke through on the French right flank and Wellington's forces repelled Napoleon's last reserve — the Imperial Guard — the French army was routed [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

The consequences were sweeping: **Napoleon abdicated just four days later**, coalition forces entered Paris on 7 July, and the battle marked the end of the First French Empire and Napoleon's Hundred Days return from exile [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo). The phrase *"meeting one's Waterloo"* has since entered popular culture as an expression for a catastrophic and final defeat [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Battle_of_Waterloo).

Results.Query: Did the Titanic sink on its first voyage?
Results.ActualAnswer: Yes. The RMS Titanic sank during its **maiden (first) voyage**. It departed Southampton on **10 April 1912** and sank in the early hours of **15 April 1912** after striking an iceberg, never completing its journey to New York [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Titanic) [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sinking_of_the_Titanic).

Results.Query: What was the price of the Apple AirPower charging mat?
Results.ActualAnswer:
The Apple AirPower was **never officially sold**, so it had no official retail price. It was announced on September 12, 2017, and was intended to be released in early 2018, but Apple canceled the product on March 29, 2019, before it ever went on sale [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AirPower_(Apple)).

The AirPower was designed to wirelessly charge up to three devices simultaneously — an iPhone, AirPods, and an Apple Watch — but it was scrapped due to technical challenges that prevented it from reaching market [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AirPower_(Apple)).

Results.Query: Did humans and dinosaurs ever co-exist?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**No** — humans and non-avian dinosaurs never co-existed.

- **Non-avian dinosaurs went extinct ~66 million years ago**, wiped out by the Cretaceous–Paleogene extinction event [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cretaceous%E2%80%93Paleogene_extinction_event).
- ***Homo sapiens* first appeared approximately 315,000 years ago** in Africa [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Timeline_of_prehistory).

That leaves a gap of roughly **65.7 million years** between the last non-avian dinosaurs and the first modern humans. As Wikipedia's list of common misconceptions states directly: *"humans and non-avian dinosaurs did not coexist at any point"* [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_common_misconceptions_about_science,_technology,_and_mathematics).

**Important nuance — birds *are* dinosaurs:** Avian dinosaurs (birds) survived the extinction event and do coexist with humans today [3](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_common_misconceptions_about_science,_technology,_and_mathematics). So in a strict scientific sense, humans *do* live alongside a lineage of dinosaurs — just not the large, non-avian ones popularly imagined.

Results.Query: What is the nationality of the artist who painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?
Results.ActualAnswer: The ceiling of the Sistine Chapel was painted in fresco by **Michelangelo** between 1508 and 1512 [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sistine_Chapel_ceiling). Michelangelo (1475–1564) was **Italian** — a sculptor, painter, and architect of the High Renaissance [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_people_from_Italy).

Results.Query: What country was the inventor of the World Wide Web born in?
Results.ActualAnswer: The inventor of the World Wide Web is **Tim Berners-Lee**, who is described as an **English** computer scientist [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_Wide_Web). He was therefore born in **England** (United Kingdom).

Results.Query: Who is the president of the country that won the 2024 T20 World Cup?
Results.ActualAnswer:
**India** won the 2024 ICC Men's T20 World Cup, defeating South Africa by seven runs in the final on 29 June 2024. It was India's second T20 World Cup title, and they became the first team to win it while remaining undefeated throughout the tournament. [1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2024_Men's_T20_World_Cup)

The President of India is **Droupadi Murmu**, the 15th and current president, who took office on 25 July 2022. [2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_India)
